ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: A collection of coding functions as alternatives to the standard functions in the stats package, which have names starting with 'contr.'.  Their main advantage is that they provide a consistent method for defining marginal effects in factorial models.  In a simple one-way ANOVA model the intercept term is always the simple average of the class means.

A collection of coding functions as alternatives to the standard functions in the stats package, which have names starting with 'contr.'.  Their main advantage is that they provide a consistent method for defining marginal effects in factorial models.  In a simple one-way ANOVA model the intercept term is always the simple average of the class means.

name: r-codingmatrices
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '0.4.0' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-codingmatrices
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/codingMatrices_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/codingMatrices/codingMatrices_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 4f3106639fee03118e4e78f9a38e44f7221ead34c213b789c7ed43e1f992a414

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-matrix
    - r-fractional
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-matrix
    - r-fractional

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('codingMatrices')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('codingMatrices')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=codingMatrices
  license: GPL-2.0-only
  summary: A collection of coding functions as alternatives to the standard functions in the
    stats package, which have names starting with 'contr.'.  Their main advantage is
    that they provide a consistent method for defining marginal effects in factorial
    models.  In a simple one-way ANOVA model the intercept term is always the simple
    average of the class means.
  license_family: GPL2
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-2'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: codingMatrices
# Type: Package
# Title: Alternative Factor Coding Matrices for Linear Model Formulae
# Version: 0.4.0
# Author: Bill Venables
# Maintainer: Bill Venables <Bill.Venables@gmail.com>
# Description: A collection of coding functions as alternatives to the standard functions in the stats package, which have names starting with 'contr.'.  Their main advantage is that they provide a consistent method for defining marginal effects in factorial models.  In a simple one-way ANOVA model the intercept term is always the simple average of the class means.
# Depends: R (>= 3.5.0), stats
# Imports: Matrix, methods, fractional, utils
# Suggests: knitr, MASS, dplyr, car, ggplot2, xtable
# License: GPL (>= 2)
# Encoding: UTF-8
# RoxygenNote: 7.2.3
# VignetteBuilder: knitr
# NeedsCompilation: no
# Packaged: 2023-02-01 07:23:22 UTC; bill
# Repository: CRAN
# Date/Publication: 2023-02-01 08:40:02 UTC
